original_query: select s_name, s_address from supplier, nation where s_suppkey in ( select ps_suppkey from partsupp where ps_partkey in ( select p_partkey from part where p_name like 'burlywood%' ) and ps_availqty > ( select 0.5 * sum(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey and l_shipdate >= date '1994-01-01' and l_shipdate < date '1994-01-01' + interval '1' year ) ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'ETHIOPIA' order by s_name;
rewritten_query:
WITH part_filtered AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_name LIKE 'burlywood%'
), 
lineitem_agg AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, l_suppkey, 0.5 * SUM(l_quantity) AS half_total_quantity
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_shipdate >= DATE '1994-01-01'
      AND l_shipdate < DATE '1995-01-01'
    GROUP BY l_partkey, l_suppkey
)
SELECT s_name, s_address
FROM supplier
JOIN nation ON supplier.s_nationkey = nation.n_nationkey
WHERE n_name = 'ETHIOPIA'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM partsupp
    INNER JOIN part_filtered ON partsupp.ps_partkey = part_filtered.p_partkey
    INNER JOIN lineitem_agg 
        ON partsupp.ps_partkey = lineitem_agg.l_partkey 
        AND partsupp.ps_suppkey = lineitem_agg.l_suppkey
    WHERE partsupp.ps_suppkey = supplier.s_suppkey
      AND partsupp.ps_availqty > lineitem_agg.half_total_quantity
  )
ORDER BY s_name;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced nested correlated subqueries with CTEs to precompute filtered parts and aggregated lineitem quantities
Converted implicit joins to explicit JOIN syntax and replaced IN clauses with EXISTS and JOINs
Precomputed the aggregation on lineitem to avoid repeated calculations for each partsupp row
Moved the date range filter to the CTE to reduce the number of rows before aggregation
